content.inputFormat: The first line contains an integer T, the number of test cases. Each of the next T lines contains two space‑separated integers A and B.
content.outputFormat: For each test case output a single character on its own line: '<' if A < B, '>' if A > B, or '=' if A = B.

sampleTestCases.explanation: First pair: 10<20 → '<'. Second pair: 20>10 → '>'. Third pair: 10=10 → '='.
Both numbers are equal, so output '='.

solution.approach: Read the number of test cases. For each case read A and B and compare them using the standard relational operators. Print the corresponding symbol. The algorithm is O(T) time and O(1) extra space.
solution.timeComplexity: O(T) – one constant‑time comparison per test case.
solution.spaceComplexity: O(1) – only a few integer variables are used.
